---
title: "ACQUIRE Framework"
subtitle: "Acquisition, Curation and QUality for In-the-wild REproducible studies"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.acq-hero}

::: {.acq-lede}
Reproducibility begins at acquisition.
:::

Most in-the-wild sensing studies collect invalid data for weeks before anyone
notices. Dashboards stay green, files keep arriving, and the failure only
surfaces during analysis — when the fieldwork is already spent.

ACQUIRE is a guideline book and a set of diagnostics for catching those failures
while they are still cheap to fix.

::: {.acq-cta}
[Building a study? Start here](lifecycle/index.qmd)
[Something wrong with your data?](recipes/index.qmd){.secondary}
:::

:::

## What silent failure looks like {.unnumbered}

Below is one hour of accelerometer data from a phone that reported itself
healthy for the entire recording. The app requested 50 Hz. Every file arrived.
No error was logged.

```{python}
#| label: fig-hero
#| fig-cap: "Effective sampling rate collapses halfway through the recording. Nothing in the application's own telemetry registers this."
#| echo: false

import acqsite, acquire
from acquire.synth import Fault

acqsite.style()
df = acquire.synth.faulty(duration_s=3600, rate_hz=50, seed=3)

fig, ax = acqsite.new_axes(height=2.1)
acqsite.rate_trace(ax, df, nominal_hz=50)
fig.tight_layout()
```

Running the diagnostics against it takes one line:

```{python}
#| echo: true
import acquire

report = acquire.check(df, nominal_hz=50)
report
```

Two failures, both invisible to conventional monitoring, both traceable to a
specific recipe. That is the whole idea.

## Two ways in {.unnumbered}

**[The guideline book](lifecycle/index.qmd)** follows the study lifecycle —
design, instrument, pilot, deploy, monitor, curate, release. Each stage carries
an ordered checklist. Use it when you are building a study and want to know what
to do next.

**[The failure catalogue](recipes/index.qmd)** is entered by symptom. Each entry
states what you observe, what causes it, how to detect it automatically, and how
well that detection is evidenced. Use it when your data already looks wrong.

## Run it on your own data {.unnumbered}

The diagnostics are a Python package, intended to run in your own pipeline or CI
against incoming study data:

```bash
pip install acquire-framework
acquire check recordings/day01.csv --nominal 50
```

It exits non-zero on failure, so it can gate a pipeline.

There is also a [browser-based demonstration](check/index.qmd) that runs the same
code locally in your tab — nothing is uploaded, and nothing leaves your device.

## Status {.unnumbered}

Version 0.1.0. The catalogue is small and honest about it: every recipe carries
an evidence level, and most are currently at the lowest one. Replications are
the single most useful contribution — see [contributing](https://github.com/acquire-framework/acquire-framework.github.io/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
